project.name: Ludo Extra
project.description: A browser-playable Ludo game where the default ruleset is Ludo Extra Mode (aggressive/competitive) with Classic Ludo available as a chill optional ruleset.

assumptions: Initial milestone is offline/local play in a single browser session (no server-authoritative multiplayer yet).
Deterministic game logic is required early to support replays and later online validation.
Territory Captures, Ransom, and Home Column Exit are runtime toggles per match.
Default Extra Mode: Territory Captures ON, Safe squares lose immunity when Territory Captures is ON.
Overshooting into center home: token stays (does not move backward).
Ransom OFF behavior: captured token returns immediately to start (classic).

tasks.title: Bootstrap design system tokens and copy constants
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want consistent design tokens and shared copy so that the UI feels cohesive and changes are easy to maintain.
tasks.description: Create the initial token file and the copy constants entrypoint required by UI components.
acceptance_criteria: Given the repo, when I inspect the codebase, then src/styles/tokens.css exists and defines spacing, typography, color, motion, shadow, radius, and breakpoint variables.
Given the repo, when I inspect the codebase, then src/copy/index.ts exists and exports ERRORS, LABELS, EMPTY_STATES, and NOTIFICATIONS.
Given the project, when I run TypeScript type-check, then it completes without errors.

tasks.title: Create UI primitives (Button, Text, IconButton) and wire tokens
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want consistent base components so that every interaction feels predictable and accessible.
tasks.description: Introduce the smallest set of primitives needed to build the game UI without duplicating styles across screens.
acceptance_criteria: Given Button, when it is focused via keyboard, then a visible focus state is shown.
Given Button, when isLoading=true, then it is disabled and shows a loading affordance.
Given Text, when rendered with each scale size, then it matches the typography scale tokens.
Given the design system rules, when I inspect styles, then spacing and colors come from tokens (no raw values in components).

tasks.title: Create composite UI components (Card/Panel, Modal, Tooltip)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want reusable layout and overlay components so that screens are consistent and quick to build.
tasks.description: Build a small set of composite components required by game settings, help, and recap flows.
acceptance_criteria: Given Modal, when opened, then focus is trapped and Escape closes it.
Given Tooltip, when triggered by keyboard focus, then it appears and is readable by screen readers.
Given Card, when rendered, then it uses the shadow and radius token scales (no custom values).

tasks.title: Build shared pattern components (EmptyState, ErrorState, LoadingState, ConfirmDialog, Toast)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want clear loading/error/empty states so that the product always communicates what is happening and what I can do next.
tasks.description: Create shared pattern components that all features will reuse.

states_required.empty: N/A — these ARE the state components

acceptance_criteria: Given EmptyState, when action props are provided, then a CTA is rendered and keyboard-accessible.
Given ErrorState, when onRetry is provided, then retry is available and clearly labeled.
Given LoadingState, when shape variants are used, then skeletons match the loaded content shape.
Given ConfirmDialog, when variant=destructive, then destructive intent is visually and textually communicated.
Given Toast, when shown, then it supports success|error|warning|info variants and auto-dismisses after a duration.

tasks.title: Setup PWA manifest and service worker scaffolding
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to install the game to my homescreen so that it feels like a native app and works offline.
tasks.description: Create PWA manifest.json with app metadata, icons, theme colors. Setup service worker registration scaffold (implementation in later task). Configure vite-pwa-plugin or equivalent.
acceptance_criteria: Given the app, when inspected, then manifest.json is valid and contains required fields (name, short_name, icons, theme_color, background_color).
Given the app, when loaded, then service worker registers without errors.
Given Lighthouse audit, when run, then PWA category scores > 90.

tasks.title: Implement service worker for offline asset caching
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want the game to work offline after first load so that I can play without internet.
tasks.description: Implement service worker with CacheFirst strategy for static assets (JS, CSS, images, lutteurs). Add runtime caching for game state (if applicable). Handle cache updates on new deployments.
acceptance_criteria: Given first visit, when assets load, then they are cached for offline use.
Given offline mode, when revisiting, then core game UI renders without network.
Given new deployment, when user revisits, then cache updates automatically (or prompts for refresh).
Given lutteurs images, when offline, then they are available from cache.

tasks.title: Define game state model and invariants for Classic + Extra rules
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want the game to behave consistently so that outcomes are fair and predictable.
tasks.description: Define TypeScript types for players, tokens, positions, dice history, move history, prisoners (ransom), rule toggles, and turn state. Document invariants in code via types and runtime checks.
acceptance_criteria: Given a new match, when initialized, then the state includes players, tokens, activePlayerId, rule toggles, and a move log.
Given token positions, when invalid (e.g., two colors stacked together), then validation fails.
Given Extra Mode toggles, when disabled, then state does not include ransom prison data (or it is always empty).

tasks.title: Implement deterministic dice roll and bonus-roll rules
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want dice rolls and bonus turns to follow the rules so that the game is fair.
tasks.description: Implement dice roll generation (seedable for replays) and apply rules: roll 6 grants extra roll, max 3 consecutive 6s with third canceled.
acceptance_criteria: Given a seeded RNG, when rolling dice N times, then the sequence is reproducible.
Given two consecutive 6s, when the next roll is 6, then the third 6 is canceled per rules.
Given a non-6 roll, when applied, then the bonus roll queue is cleared.

tasks.title: Implement movement and path validation (Classic rules)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want token movement to always be legal so that the game cannot enter invalid states.
tasks.description: Implement movement rules: starting a token only on 6, clockwise movement on common path, entering home column on completing loop, exact landing into center home.
acceptance_criteria: Given a token in start, when roll != 6, then no start move is generated.
Given a token at home-entry boundary, when it completes a loop, then it transitions into its home column.
Given a token inside the home column, when roll overshoots the center, then the configured variant behavior applies (stay vs move backward).

tasks.title: Implement capturing and safe-zone behavior (Classic + toggle for territory captures)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want captures and safe squares to work correctly so that strategy is meaningful.
tasks.description: Implement capture resolution (exact landing) and safe zone logic. When Territory Captures is ON, safe squares lose immunity.
acceptance_criteria: Given a move landing on an opponent token, when safe-zone immunity applies, then capture does not occur.
Given Territory Captures enabled, when landing on an opponent token on a safe square, then capture occurs.
Given a capture, when resolved, then the capturing player receives a bonus roll.

tasks.title: Implement Extra Mode advanced rules (territory captures, stacking, ransom, home-column exit)
tasks.user_story: As a competitive player, I want Extra Mode mechanics so that the game is more aggressive and strategic.
tasks.description: Implement: captures inside home columns (territory captures), stack formation and capture-as-a-unit, ransom prisoner lifecycle, and home column exit on 6.
acceptance_criteria: Given a stack (2+ same-color tokens), when an opponent lands exactly on the square, then all tokens in the stack are captured together.
Given Ransom enabled, when a capture occurs, then the token becomes a prisoner instead of immediately returning to start.
Given a player rolls 6 with prisoners, when they choose retrieval, then exactly one prisoner is returned according to the rules.
Given a token in its home column, when roll=6 and home-column exit is enabled, then the token exits to the home-column entry square.

tasks.title: Create turn reducer and action processing (server-authoritative ready)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want the game to accept only valid actions so that cheating and desync are prevented.
tasks.description: Implement an action-based reducer: RollDice, SelectToken, CommitMove, ToggleRules (pre-match only), etc. The reducer must validate and produce deterministic next state.
acceptance_criteria: Given an invalid action, when applied, then it is rejected with a clear error code.
Given valid actions, when applied, then the resulting state matches the rules engine outputs.
Given the same initial state + actions, when replayed, then the final state is identical.

tasks.title: Define replay and move log format and implement serialization
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want replays so that I can review key moments and share matches later.
tasks.description: Define a replay format (seed + action list + metadata) and implement serialization/deserialization.
acceptance_criteria: Given a finished game, when exporting replay, then it includes initial seed and action list.
Given an exported replay, when importing, then the game can be reconstructed deterministically.
Given an imported replay, when validated, then invalid action sequences are rejected.

tasks.title: Define board coordinate system and path mapping
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want tokens to move along the correct path so that the board representation matches the rules.
tasks.description: Create a canonical mapping for: 52 common squares, 4 starting squares, home columns, and center home. Provide helpers to convert logical positions to render coordinates.
acceptance_criteria: Given a token logical position, when mapped, then a stable render coordinate is produced.
Given each color, when mapping home column squares, then indices are consistent (0..4).
Given a full loop, when enumerating path squares, then it contains exactly 52 unique common squares.

tasks.title: Build interactive board UI (render board, tokens, safe squares)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to see and interact with the board so that I can play the game.
tasks.description: Create the core Board component that renders the cross-shaped layout with quadrants, tokens, safe squares visuals, and basic hit targets.

states_required.empty: Pre-game board with tokens in home/start areas

acceptance_criteria: Given a match state, when rendered, then all tokens appear at the correct logical squares.
Given a token, when clicked (if selectable), then it triggers selection UX (highlight/preview).
Given a responsive viewport, when resized, then the board remains usable at mobile and desktop sizes.
definition_of_done: Accessibility floor met (keyboard focus where applicable)

tasks.title: Add token movement animations and capture effects
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want animated moves so that the game feels responsive and understandable.
tasks.description: Animate token movement (transform/opacity only). Add capture animations/effects and a subtle celebration for reaching home.

states_required.error: ErrorState for animation failures (fallback to instant move)

acceptance_criteria: Given a committed move, when applied, then token transitions between squares using transform animations.
Given reduced-motion preference, when enabled, then animations are minimized or disabled.
Given a capture, when resolved, then a clear capture effect plays without blocking input longer than necessary.

tasks.title: Implement visual hints (valid moves, threatened tokens, capture paths)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want visual hints so that I can quickly understand my options and risks.
tasks.description: Highlight valid moves for the current roll, threatened tokens that can be captured next turn, and optional capture-path previews.
acceptance_criteria: Given a roll, when there are valid moves, then eligible tokens/squares are highlighted.
Given there are no valid moves, when the player tries to act, then an explanation is shown.
Given opponent positions, when computing threats, then threatened tokens are indicated.
definition_of_done: Copy is sourced from shared copy constants

tasks.title: Build the main Game screen shell (HUD, turn indicator, move log)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want a clear game HUD so that I always know whose turn it is and what just happened.
tasks.description: Assemble a Game screen with Board, player list, turn indicator, dice panel placeholder, and move history/log.

states_required.empty: Pre-game state with Start Game CTA
states_required.error: ErrorState when game state fails to initialize

acceptance_criteria: Given a match, when the active player changes, then the HUD updates immediately.
Given a move, when committed, then it is appended to the move log.
Given the UI rules, when strings are shown, then they come from shared copy constants.

tasks.title: Implement dice roll interaction + animation + triple-6 feedback
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want a satisfying dice roll interaction so that the game feels alive and the rules are obvious.
tasks.description: Build a Dice component that triggers engine roll action, displays results, and communicates bonus-roll and triple-6 cancellation.

states_required.error: Toast error for invalid roll attempt

acceptance_criteria: Given it is not the player's turn, when clicking dice, then the roll action is blocked with explanation.
Given a roll of 6, when resolved, then bonus roll is indicated.
Given a third consecutive 6, when rolled, then the cancellation is clearly communicated.

tasks.title: Implement move selection UX (token selection, preview, commit)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want clear move selection so that I don't make accidental moves.
tasks.description: Allow selecting a token, previewing the destination, and committing the move. Ensure no accidental commits on touch devices.
acceptance_criteria: Given a roll, when selecting a token, then a preview shows the destination square.
Given multiple legal moves, when switching tokens, then preview updates.
Given a commit action, when applied, then engine-validated move is executed and logged.
definition_of_done: No useEffect-based workflow logic (use explicit state/reducer)

tasks.title: Build match setup + ruleset toggles (Classic vs Extra + per-rule switches)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to choose Classic or Extra mode and rule toggles so that I can control the experience.
tasks.description: Create a pre-game setup flow: player count, mode selection, and toggles (territory captures, ransom, home-column exit, overshoot behavior).
acceptance_criteria: Given Classic mode, when selected, then Extra-only toggles are disabled or hidden.
Given Extra mode, when selected, then territory captures, ransom, and home exit toggles are available.
Given invalid setup, when attempting to start, then the user sees exactly what to fix.

tasks.title: Implement end-of-game recap (placements + key stats)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want a recap so that I can understand how the match played out.
tasks.description: Show winner/placements and basic stats: captures, longest 6 streak, tokens finished, prisoners ransomed (if enabled).

states_required.error: ErrorState if recap cannot be computed

acceptance_criteria: Given a game ends, when recap opens, then placements are correct.
Given extra mode with ransom, when enabled, then prisoner stats reflect the match.
Given recap, when choosing rematch, then a new match is started with the same settings (unless changed).
definition_of_done: ConfirmDialog used for destructive actions (e.g., abandon match)

tasks.title: Build PWA install prompt component
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to be prompted to install the app after my first game so that I can easily add it to my homescreen.
tasks.description: Create a custom PWA install prompt that appears after first game completion. Uses beforeinstallprompt event. Highlights benefits: offline play, notifications, quick access.
acceptance_criteria: Given first game completion, when prompt triggers, then it appears with benefits explanation.
Given user accepts, when installed, then the prompt disappears and app launches from homescreen.
Given user dismisses, when dismissed, then prompt respects dismissal and doesn't reappear immediately.
Given already installed, when visiting, then prompt does not show.

tasks.title: Local multiplayer seat setup (2-4 players, colors, quick mode tokens)
tasks.user_story: As a group of players, I want local setup so that we can play together on one device.
tasks.description: Support 2-4 players with color assignment and optional quick mode (2 tokens per player).
acceptance_criteria: Given 2-4 seats, when configured, then match can start.
Given duplicate color selection, when attempted, then it is prevented with explanation.
Given quick mode, when enabled, then the match initializes with 2 tokens per player.

tasks.title: Implement Easy AI (random valid move)
tasks.user_story: As a solo player, I want an easy AI so that I can practice quickly.
tasks.description: Choose randomly among valid actions/moves with simple tie-breakers.
acceptance_criteria: Given a turn with valid moves, when AI acts, then it selects a legal move.
Given no valid moves, when AI acts, then it skips appropriately according to rules.
Given deterministic mode (seed), when replaying, then AI decisions are reproducible.

tasks.title: Implement Medium AI (basic heuristics: capture priority, safety, finishing)
tasks.user_story: As a solo player, I want a medium AI so that matches feel competitive.
tasks.description: Heuristics: prefer captures, avoid immediate threats, prefer bringing tokens out, prefer finishing when close to home, consider stack safety in Extra mode.
acceptance_criteria: Given an available capture, when safe, then AI prefers capture over non-capture.
Given a move that exposes a token to immediate capture, when alternatives exist, then AI avoids it.
Given finishing is possible, when it does not sacrifice a high-value capture, then AI prioritizes finishing.

tasks.title: Build PlayerAvatar component with lutteurs fallback
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to see player avatars so that I can easily identify opponents and feel more engaged.
tasks.description: Create a PlayerAvatar component that displays a player's image. Uses authenticated profile image if available; falls back to rotating lutteurs images (balla-gueye, eumeu-sene, franc, modou-lo) for unauthenticated/local play.
acceptance_criteria: Given authenticated user, when avatar renders, then profile image is displayed.
Given unauthenticated user, when avatar renders, then a lutteurs image is assigned consistently per player.
Given image load failure, when error occurs, then a fallback icon is shown.
Given reduced motion preference, when animating, then avatar transitions respect the preference.

tasks.title: Draft online architecture spec (server-authoritative state + anti-cheat)
tasks.user_story: As an online player, I want fair matches so that cheating and desync are prevented.
tasks.description: Write a minimal spec for server-authoritative action processing, deterministic dice generation, reconnect grace, and idempotent action handling.
acceptance_criteria: Given the spec, when reviewed, then it defines inputs/outputs for client actions and server responses.
Given reconnect, when a client resumes, then the spec explains how to resync state.
Given duplicate actions, when received, then the spec defines idempotency behavior.

tasks.title: Define multiplayer room protocol (WebSocket message shapes)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want private rooms so that I can play with friends.
tasks.description: Define message types for room creation/join, state sync, action submission, and minimal chat stubs.
acceptance_criteria: Given a room, when a player joins, then the protocol specifies handshake + full state sync.
Given actions, when sent, then the protocol specifies acknowledgements and error codes.
Given chat, when used, then the protocol defines message payload shape.

tasks.title: Define ranked points + leaderboard data model (future)
tasks.user_story: As a competitive player, I want rankings so that I can track my progress.
tasks.description: Specify how points are awarded per placement, how seasons reset, and what stats are stored per player.
acceptance_criteria: Given placements, when computing points, then the model matches the rules doc (+500/+250/+100/+50).
Given a season reset, when applied, then the spec defines what persists and what resets.
Given leaderboards, when queried, then the spec defines pagination and caching strategy at a high level.

tasks.title: Implement win condition detection and placement tracking (1st–4th)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want the game to correctly detect when I win and track final placements so that the recap is accurate.
tasks.description: Detect when a player has all 4 tokens in center home. Record finishing order. Emit game-over event when all positions decided. Integrate with turn reducer.
acceptance_criteria: Given a player whose 4th token reaches center home, when evaluated, then that player is assigned the next available placement.
Given 4 players, when all finish, then a game-over action is dispatched with final placements array.
Given a 2-player match, when the first player finishes, then the second is immediately assigned 2nd place and game ends.
Given game-over dispatched, when any further move action is attempted, then it is rejected.

tasks.title: Implement rule variants: double-block, no-bonus-6, three-6s-penalty, quick-start
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want optional rule variants so that I can customize the match experience.
tasks.description: Toggleable rule variant flags: double-block (two same-color tokens block square), no-bonus-6 (rolling 6 grants no extra roll), three-6s-penalty (third 6 sends token to start), quick-start (tokens begin on starting squares without needing a 6).
acceptance_criteria: Given double-block enabled, when two same-color tokens share a square, then no other player can land on it.
Given no-bonus-6 enabled, when rolling 6, then no extra roll is granted.
Given three-6s-penalty enabled, when the third consecutive 6 is rolled, then the last moved token returns to start.
Given quick-start enabled, when match starts, then all tokens begin on starting squares without requiring a 6.
out_of_scope: UI for toggling variants (handled in match setup)

tasks.title: Implement game state persistence (save/load via localStorage)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want my game saved automatically so that I can resume if I accidentally close the tab.
tasks.description: Serialize full game state to localStorage after every action. Load and resume on app launch if a saved game exists. Handle corrupt or stale saves gracefully.
acceptance_criteria: Given an active match, when any action is committed, then state is persisted to localStorage.
Given a saved game, when the app is reopened, then the player is offered to resume or start new.
Given a corrupt or version-mismatched save, when detected, then it is discarded silently.
Given the user starts a new game while one is saved, when confirmed, then the old save is cleared.

tasks.title: Build contextual rule tooltips ('Why can't I move?', 'Why was I captured?')
tasks.user_story: As a new player, I want contextual explanations for rule-driven events so that I understand what just happened without leaving the game.
tasks.description: Surface inline tooltips or brief overlays explaining: blocked move, capture reason, triple-6 cancel, ransom hold, territory capture in home column.

states_required.success: Explanation shown contextually after triggering event

acceptance_criteria: Given a player taps a token with no valid moves, when evaluated, then a tooltip explains why (e.g. 'No valid moves for this roll').
Given a token is captured, when resolved, then the captured player sees a brief explanation.
Given triple-6 cancellation, when it triggers, then an overlay explains 'Third 6 cancelled — your turn ends'.
Given ransom mode with a token taken prisoner, when it occurs, then the ransom rule is briefly explained.
Given the explanation, when dismissed, then the game continues without interruption.
definition_of_done: Copy sourced from shared copy constants

tasks.title: Implement winning celebration and placement finish animations
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want a satisfying celebration when I win and clear feedback for all placements.
tasks.description: Display win celebration overlay for 1st place and placement badges for 2nd–4th. Transform/opacity animations only. Reduced motion must be respected. Transitions to recap screen.

states_required.success: Celebration plays then transitions to recap

acceptance_criteria: Given a player gets their 4th token home, when detected, then a win celebration overlay appears.
Given 2nd/3rd/4th place finishes, when each occurs, then a placement badge is shown briefly before game continues.
Given reduced-motion preference, when enabled, then animations are replaced with instant state changes.
Given the celebration, when dismissed or auto-timed out, then the recap screen is shown.

tasks.title: Build emotes/taunts panel (non-toxic preset reactions)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to send quick emotes during the game so that matches feel social and expressive.
tasks.description: Preset non-toxic emote panel. Emotes display as brief floating bubbles near the sender's player area. Cooldown prevents spam.
acceptance_criteria: Given a player opens the emote panel, when selecting an emote, then it appears as a bubble near their player area.
Given an emote sent, when displayed, then it auto-dismisses after a short duration.
Given emotes used, when displayed, then they are sourced from a curated non-toxic preset list (no custom text).
Given rapid emote sending, when detected, then a cooldown prevents spam.
definition_of_done: Emote copy from shared copy constants

tasks.title: Build onboarding: interactive first-time experience for Extra Mode rules
tasks.user_story: As a new player, I want a guided introduction to Extra Mode so that I understand the advanced rules before my first match.
tasks.description: Interactive onboarding flow walking through Extra Mode rules: territory captures, stacking, ransom, home column exit. Uses real board component with guided overlays.

states_required.success: User completes onboarding and proceeds to match setup

acceptance_criteria: Given a first-time player, when they open the app, then they are offered the Extra Mode onboarding.
Given the onboarding flow, when proceeding through steps, then each rule is demonstrated on the live board.
Given a player wants to skip, when skipping, then they go directly to match setup.
Given onboarding completed, when returning to the app, then onboarding is not shown again (localStorage flag).
definition_of_done: All 4 Extra Mode rules covered (territory captures, stacking, ransom, home column exit)

tasks.title: Build replay viewer UI
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to watch a replay of a past match so that I can review key moments.
tasks.description: Replay viewer screen: load serialized replay, replay actions step by step, playback controls (play/pause, step forward/back, speed selector).
acceptance_criteria: Given a valid replay, when loaded, then the board reconstructs the match from first action.
Given play mode, when running, then actions execute at 1x speed with 0.5x/2x options.
Given step controls, when used, then the player can advance or rewind one action at a time.
Given an invalid replay, when loaded, then an error state explains the issue.
Given reduced motion, when enabled, then replay transitions are instant.

tasks.title: Implement accessibility: color-blind mode and haptics toggles
tasks.user_story: As a player with visual impairments, I want color-blind mode and haptic options so that the game is fully playable for me.
tasks.description: Color-blind mode (pattern/icon overlays on tokens and squares in addition to color), haptics toggle (vibration on capture/dice roll via navigator.vibrate on mobile), persisted to localStorage.
acceptance_criteria: Given color-blind mode enabled, when viewing the board, then tokens have shape/pattern distinctions in addition to color.
Given haptics enabled on mobile, when a capture or dice roll occurs, then a vibration fires via navigator.vibrate.
Given accessibility settings changed, when saved, then they are persisted to localStorage and applied on reload.
Given reduced motion OS preference, when set, then all animations respect it without any manual toggle required.
definition_of_done: Color-blind mode tested with deuteranopia simulation

tasks.title: Build highlight reel: auto-generate best moments from a match
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want the game to surface the best moments automatically so that I can relive and share highlights.
tasks.description: After match end, identify highlight moments from the move log (captures, triple-stacks, ransom retrievals, home column exits) and present them in the recap as a highlight reel.
acceptance_criteria: Given a finished match, when recap opens, then moments are classified from the move log.
Given highlights displayed, when shown, then each moment links to the replay at that timestep via seek.
Given no notable events, when generating, then a graceful empty state is shown.

tasks.title: Implement shareable match/replay links with privacy controls
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to share a match replay link so that friends can watch what happened.
tasks.description: Generate shareable URL encoding the replay. Include privacy controls (public/unlisted). Integrate with Web Share API on mobile.

supporting_elements: Web Share API sheet on mobile

acceptance_criteria: Given a finished match, when share is triggered, then a link encoding the replay is generated.
Given Web Share API availability on mobile, then the native share sheet is used.
Given privacy set to unlisted, when shared, then the link works but the match is not discoverable.
Given a shared link, when opened, then the replay viewer loads and plays.
out_of_scope: Server-side replay storage (use localStorage encoding for v1)

tasks.title: Implement audio system foundation (AudioManager, volume controls, mute)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want audio feedback so that the game feels alive, with the ability to control or mute sounds.
tasks.description: Create an AudioManager singleton loading and playing sounds via Web Audio API. Volume control and mute toggle. Persist preferences to localStorage.
acceptance_criteria: Given the AudioManager, when initialized, then it loads sound assets without blocking rendering.
Given muted state, when events fire, then no sounds play.
Given volume 0–100, when a sound plays, then it respects the configured level.
Given audio preferences, when persisted, then they are restored on next launch.

tasks.title: Implement game sound effects (dice roll, token move, capture, win)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want sound effects for key game events so that the game feels satisfying.
tasks.description: Wire game events to AudioManager: dice roll, token move, capture, and win celebration. Source minimal royalty-free assets.
acceptance_criteria: Given a dice roll event, when it fires, then the dice roll sound plays.
Given a token move event, when it fires, then the move sound plays.
Given a capture event, when it fires, then the capture sound plays.
Given a win event, when it fires, then the win celebration sound plays.
Given audio muted, when any event fires, then no sound is emitted.

tasks.title: Implement background music (looping, fade in/out, separate volume)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want optional background music so that the game atmosphere feels immersive.
tasks.description: Looping background music track via AudioManager. Fade in on match start, fade out on game end. Separate music volume from SFX volume.
acceptance_criteria: Given match start, when board renders, then background music fades in over 1s.
Given game over, when triggered, then music fades out over 1s.
Given music muted, when toggled, then music stops immediately.
Given separate music/SFX volume sliders, when adjusted, then they are fully independent.
out_of_scope: Dynamic music based on game state

tasks.title: Build audio settings UI (SFX volume, music volume, mute toggles)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to control sound settings so that I can customize my audio experience.
tasks.description: Audio settings panel accessible from game HUD and main settings. SFX volume slider, music volume slider, and individual mute toggles.
acceptance_criteria: Given the audio settings panel, when adjusting SFX volume, then a preview sound plays at the new level.
Given the mute toggle, when enabled, then all audio is suppressed.
Given settings changed, when saved, then they persist to localStorage.
Given the panel opened mid-match, when interacted with, then the game is not interrupted.

tasks.title: Implement Hard AI (optimal pathfinding, capture priority, stack awareness)
tasks.user_story: As a competitive solo player, I want a hard AI that plays near-optimally so that I face a real challenge.
tasks.description: Hard AI using heuristic search: maximize captures, minimize exposure, prioritize finishing, build stacks defensively, consider ransom retrieval timing.
acceptance_criteria: Given a position where capturing is optimal, when evaluated, then Hard AI selects the capture move.
Given a finishing opportunity that doesn't sacrifice a high-value position, when evaluated, then Hard AI finalizes the token.
Given Extra Mode with stacks, when evaluating, then Hard AI builds and protects stacks strategically.
Given Hard AI vs Medium AI in 100 simulated games, then Hard AI wins at least 70% of the time.
Given deterministic seed, when replaying, then Hard AI decisions are reproducible.

tasks.title: Implement Team Mode engine (2v2, partner immunity, shared win condition)
tasks.user_story: As a player in a group of 4, I want to play 2v2 so that I can cooperate with a partner.
tasks.description: Team mode where opposite players are partners. Win condition: both partners finish all tokens. Partners cannot capture each other.
acceptance_criteria: Given team mode enabled, when initialized, then Red+Green are Team A and Yellow+Blue are Team B.
Given partners on the same square, when evaluated, then no capture occurs between them.
Given a team, when both partners finish all their tokens, then that team is declared the winner.
Given single partner finishes, when the other has not, then the game continues until both are done.

tasks.title: Build Team Mode setup UI and partner indicator on board
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to see my partner clearly during a team match so that I can coordinate.
tasks.description: Team mode selection in match setup. Partner badge in HUD and on board. Differentiate partner tokens visually from opponent tokens.
acceptance_criteria: Given team mode selected, when in setup, then players are grouped into two pairs with partner labels.
Given a match in team mode, when on board, then a visual indicator distinguishes partner from opponent tokens.
Given 2 or 3 player count, when team mode is selected, then an error explains it requires exactly 4 players.

tasks.title: Implement WebSocket server (room create/join, state sync, action relay)
tasks.user_story: As an online player, I want to play with friends in real time so that distance doesn't stop us from playing.
tasks.description: WebSocket server handling room creation, player join/leave, game state broadcast, action relay. Server-authoritative state. Reconnect grace period (30s). Idempotent action processing.
acceptance_criteria: Given a room code, when a player joins, then they receive a full state sync.
Given a player action, when submitted, then the server validates and broadcasts the new state.
Given a disconnect within the 30s grace period, when rejoining, then the player resyncs.
Given an invalid action, when submitted, then the server rejects it with an error code.
Given duplicate action on reconnect, when detected, then idempotency prevents double processing.

tasks.title: Build online lobby UI (create room, join by code, player list, ready state)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to create or join a private room so that I can play with friends online.
tasks.description: Lobby screen: create room (get 6-char code), share it, wait for others. Live player list with ready states. Host starts when all are ready.

states_required.success: All players ready, host can start

acceptance_criteria: Given a player creates a room, when created, then a 6-character room code is displayed and copyable.
Given a room code entered, when joined, then the player appears in the lobby.
Given all players are ready, when host clicks Start, then the match begins.
Given a connection error, when joining, then an error state with retry is shown.

tasks.title: Implement friend list and invite management
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want a friend list so that I can quickly invite people I know.
tasks.description: Friend list screen: add friend by username, accept/decline requests, see online/in-game status, send game invite directly from friend list.
acceptance_criteria: Given a username search, when submitted, then the user can send a friend request.
Given a pending request, when accepted, then the friend appears in the list with their status.
Given an online friend, when viewing, then an 'Invite to game' button is available.
Given loading fails, when shown, then an error state with retry is displayed.

tasks.title: Implement in-game text chat with emoji reactions
tasks.user_story: As an online player, I want to text chat during a match so that I can communicate with others.
tasks.description: Collapsible in-game chat panel with message input, emoji picker, and system messages (captures, turn events) delivered via WebSocket.
acceptance_criteria: Given an online match, when chat is open, then players can send messages visible to all in the room.
Given an emoji picker, when an emoji is selected, then it appends to the message input.
Given a capture event, when it fires, then a system message appears in chat.
Given a message with prohibited content, when sent, then it is filtered client-side before sending.

tasks.title: Implement in-game voice chat (WebRTC push-to-talk and always-on)
tasks.user_story: As an online player with friends, I want voice chat so that we can talk while playing.
tasks.description: WebRTC peer-to-peer audio within a game room. Modes: push-to-talk and always-on. Voice activity detection. Per-player mute/unmute.
acceptance_criteria: Given voice chat enabled, when a room is active, then players can communicate via audio.
Given push-to-talk mode, when the button is held, then audio is transmitted.
Given always-on mode, when voice activity is detected, then audio is transmitted.
Given a player muted, when evaluating, then their audio is not transmitted to others.
Given poor connection, when audio degrades, then it silences gracefully rather than corrupting.

tasks.title: Build voice chat UI controls (mute, mode toggle, speaking indicators)
tasks.user_story: As an online player using voice chat, I want clear controls so that I can manage who speaks and when.
tasks.description: Voice chat controls in game HUD: mute/unmute self, switch PTT/always-on, visual speaking indicators per player avatar.
acceptance_criteria: Given voice connected, when a player speaks, then a speaking indicator appears near their avatar.
Given mute button pressed, when toggled, then self-mute is applied and indicator reflects the state.
Given microphone permission denied, when voice is attempted, then an error state guides the user to settings.
Given push-to-talk, when the button is released, then transmission stops immediately.

tasks.title: Implement spectator mode
tasks.user_story: As a player watching a friend's match, I want spectator mode so that I can observe without interfering.
tasks.description: Users join a room as spectator (view-only). Full board state in real time, no action controls. Spectator count shown to players.

states_required.error: Room not found / spectators not allowed

acceptance_criteria: Given a spectator link, when joined, then the user sees the board without action controls.
Given a spectator, when game state updates, then their view updates in real time.
Given players, when spectators are present, then a spectator count badge is visible.
Given spectators disabled for a room, when a link is used, then an error explains the restriction.

tasks.title: Implement quick match / skill-based matchmaking
tasks.user_story: As a solo player, I want to be matched with others of similar skill so that matches are fair.
tasks.description: Matchmaking queue using ELO range matching. Support 2–4 player lobbies. Cancel queue, estimated wait time, timeout fallback to wider range.
acceptance_criteria: Given a player in queue, when a match is found, then all players are placed in a lobby automatically.
Given ELO matching, when initial range finds no match within 15s, then the range widens.
Given cancel queue, when triggered, then the player exits cleanly.
Given 4 players found, when lobby is filled, then the match starts automatically.

tasks.title: Build matchmaking queue UI (searching, cancel, match found)
tasks.user_story: As a player searching for a match, I want to see my queue status so that I know what's happening.
tasks.description: Matchmaking screen: animated searching state, estimated wait time, cancel button, match found overlay with countdown.
acceptance_criteria: Given searching state, when active, then an animation communicates the queue is live.
Given a match found, when triggered, then a 'Match Found!' overlay appears with a countdown to accept.
Given cancel pressed, when triggered, then the queue is abandoned and user returns to main menu.
Given a timeout, when the server fails to find a match, then an error state with retry is shown.

tasks.title: Implement Clubs/Guilds (create, join, shared chat, club leaderboard)
tasks.user_story: As a social player, I want to join a club so that I can compete and chat with a community.
tasks.description: Create or join clubs. Each club has shared chat, member list, club-internal leaderboard. Club admins can invite or remove members.

states_required.success: Club home with chat and leaderboard

acceptance_criteria: Given a player creating a club, when created, then they become admin and receive a club code.
Given a club code, when joined, then the player appears in the member list.
Given a club leaderboard, when viewed, then members are ranked by global ranking points.
Given a club admin removing a member, when confirmed, then that member loses club access immediately.

tasks.title: Implement party queue (matchmake as a group of 2–3 friends)
tasks.user_story: As a player with friends, I want to queue as a party so that we are placed in the same match.
tasks.description: 2–3 players form a party and enter quick match together. Party leader controls queue. Remaining slots filled by matchmaking. ELO average used for skill matching.
acceptance_criteria: Given a party of 2, when queuing, then they are placed in the same match and remaining slots filled by matchmaking.
Given the party leader cancels, when triggered, then all party members exit the queue.
Given a party member disconnects pre-match, when detected, then the party is notified and can continue or disband.
Given party queue, when matching, then ELO average of the party is used.

tasks.title: Implement turn timer and AFK handling (auto-move, auto-skip) for online play
tasks.user_story: As an online player, I want AFK players handled automatically so that matches don't stall.
tasks.description: Server-enforced turn timer (default 25s online). On expiry, auto-select a valid move; if none, skip. After 3 consecutive AFK turns, flag player as disconnected and have AI take over.
acceptance_criteria: Given an online turn, when it starts, then a 25s countdown begins server-side.
Given timer expiry with valid moves, when triggered, then the server auto-selects a simple valid move.
Given timer expiry with no valid moves, when triggered, then the turn is skipped.
Given 3 consecutive AFK turns, when detected, then AI takes over for that player.
Given the player acts before expiry, when triggered, then the timer cancels cleanly.

tasks.title: Build turn timer UI and AFK countdown display
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to see the turn timer so that I know how much time I have.
tasks.description: Per-turn countdown in the HUD. Urgency animation for last 5s. AFK warning toast shown to the affected player.

states_required.success: Timer counting down with urgency states

acceptance_criteria: Given an online turn, when the timer starts, then a countdown bar is visible in the HUD.
Given last 5 seconds, when remaining, then the timer changes color/pulses to indicate urgency.
Given the current player is AFK, when 3s remain, then a 'You are about to be skipped' warning appears.
Given reduced motion, when enabled, then the urgency pulse is replaced with a color change only.

tasks.title: Implement player reporting and block list
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to report toxic players and block them so that I feel safe in the community.
tasks.description: Report flow (toxic behavior, cheating, spam) accessible from in-game player context menu. Block list management in settings. Blocked players cannot join the same room.
acceptance_criteria: Given a player context menu, when report is tapped, then a modal shows report categories.
Given a report submitted, when confirmed, then a success toast is shown and the report is sent.
Given a player blocked, when they attempt to join the same room, then they are denied entry.
Given the block list in settings, when viewed, then blocked players can be unblocked.

tasks.title: Implement points-based ranking system and player profile data model
tasks.user_story: As a competitive player, I want a ranking system so that wins feel meaningful and I can track my progress.
tasks.description: Define and implement the player profile data model: ELO score, rank tier, placement points, season points, stats (wins, losses, captures, games played). Implement point award logic (+500/+250/+100/+50 by placement). Define rank tier thresholds (Bronze → Grandmaster).
acceptance_criteria: Given a player finishing 1st, when points are calculated, then +500 is awarded.
Given a player finishing 4th, when points are calculated, then +50 is awarded.
Given point totals, when mapped to tiers, then thresholds correctly produce Bronze/Silver/Gold/Platinum/Diamond/Master/Grandmaster.
Given a player profile, when queried, then it returns ELO, rank tier, season points, and stats.
definition_of_done: Tier thresholds documented as named constants

tasks.title: Implement ELO matchmaking algorithm and point decay
tasks.user_story: As a competitive player, I want ELO-based matching so that I face opponents of similar skill.
tasks.description: ELO calculation for 4-player matches (multi-player ELO adaptation). Point decay for inactive players (e.g. -10 pts/week after 14 days inactive). Decay never drops a player below their tier floor.
acceptance_criteria: Given a 4-player match result, when ELO is updated, then each player's ELO adjusts based on placement relative to expected outcomes.
Given an inactive player (no games for 14 days), when decay runs, then their ELO decreases by the configured decay rate.
Given point decay, when applied, then it never drops a player below their tier floor.
Given two players of equal ELO, when matched, then expected win probability is 50%.

tasks.title: Implement placement matches for new ranked players
tasks.user_story: As a new competitive player, I want placement matches so that I start at the right skill level.
tasks.description: New players complete 5 placement matches before receiving a rank. Provisional ELO tracked during placement. After 5 matches, final rank assigned and displayed.
acceptance_criteria: Given a new player entering ranked, when evaluating status, then they are in provisional status for 5 games.
Given 5 placement games completed, when evaluated, then a rank is assigned based on provisional ELO.
Given provisional status, when displayed in the UI, then a 'Placement X/5' badge replaces the rank badge.
Given placement games, when matched, then they are paired against estimated similar-skill players.
out_of_scope: Reset of placements on new season (covered in season task)

tasks.title: Implement season reset logic and rollover
tasks.user_story: As a competitive player, I want seasons to reset periodically so that rankings stay competitive and fresh.
tasks.description: Season structure (monthly or quarterly). At season end: archive season stats, soft reset ELO (compress towards midpoint), award season rewards, start new season requiring fresh placements.
acceptance_criteria: Given a season end, when triggered, then current ELO is compressed towards the midpoint (not zeroed).
Given a season end, when processed, then season stats are archived and accessible in player history.
Given new season start, when a player logs in, then they are informed of their soft-reset rank and invited to place again.
Given tier rewards, when season ends, then players receive the cosmetic/badge for their final tier.

tasks.title: Build player profile screen and leaderboards (local, global)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to see my profile stats and the leaderboard so that I can track my progress and compare with others.
tasks.description: Player profile screen: rank badge, ELO, stats (wins, captures, games), season history. Local (friends) and global leaderboards with pagination.
acceptance_criteria: Given a player profile, when viewed, then rank badge, ELO, wins, captures, and games played are shown.
Given the global leaderboard, when loaded, then top 100 players are shown with pagination.
Given the local leaderboard, when viewed, then only friends are ranked.
Given no ranked games, when profile opens, then an empty state encourages the player to try ranked.

tasks.title: Define tournament data model (bracket, participants, rounds, prizes)
tasks.user_story: As a competitive player, I want tournaments so that I can compete for prizes and prestige in bracket-style events.
tasks.description: Tournament data model: type (single/double elimination), participant list, bracket generation, round scheduling, prize pool definition.
acceptance_criteria: Given a tournament with 8 players, when bracket is generated, then a valid single-elimination bracket is produced.
Given match results, when reported, then the bracket advances correctly.
Given a prize pool, when defined, then it specifies virtual currency amounts per placement.
Given a tournament spec, when validated, then participant count matches supported bracket sizes (4, 8, 16, 32).

tasks.title: Build tournament browser and registration UI
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to browse and register for tournaments so that I can compete.
tasks.description: Tournament browser: upcoming and active tournaments with name, start time, prize pool, participant count, registration CTA. Registration within entry window.
acceptance_criteria: Given upcoming tournaments, when listed, then each shows name, start time, prize, and open slots.
Given an open tournament, when registration is clicked, then the player is registered and receives confirmation.
Given a full tournament, when viewing, then registration is disabled with a waitlist option.
Given no tournaments, when browsing, then an empty state indicates when the next event is.

tasks.title: Build live tournament bracket viewer
tasks.user_story: As a player or spectator, I want to see the live bracket so that I can follow the tournament progression.
tasks.description: Bracket viewer updating in real time as matches complete. Shows player names, results, current round. Spectate button for live matches.
acceptance_criteria: Given an active tournament, when bracket is viewed, then all rounds and matches are displayed.
Given a match result, when reported, then the bracket updates in real time.
Given a live match, when the spectate button is pressed, then the user joins as spectator.
Given the bracket on mobile, when scrolled, then it remains readable with horizontal scroll.

tasks.title: Define perks system data model and engine integration points
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want perks so that I can add a strategic power-up layer to matches.
tasks.description: Define perks data model: perk type, cooldown, activation trigger, effect, duration. Integrate perk activation/expiry hooks into the turn reducer. Define all 8 perks: Capture Immunity, Auto-Retrieval, Dice Selection, Double Move, Shield Token, Teleport, Stack Breaker, and their cooldown rules.
acceptance_criteria: Given a perk type, when defined, then it has: id, name, effect, cooldown turns, activation conditions, and expiry.
Given perk activation, when processed by the reducer, then the effect is applied and cooldown counter starts.
Given cooldown active, when a player tries to use the same perk, then it is rejected.
Given a match with perks disabled, when evaluated, then no perk actions are accepted.
definition_of_done: All 8 perks specced with effects and cooldowns

tasks.title: Implement all 8 perk effects in the game engine
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want each perk to work correctly so that they add meaningful strategic depth.
tasks.description: Implement effect logic for all 8 perks: Capture Immunity (1-turn protection), Auto-Retrieval (instant prisoner return without rolling 6), Dice Selection (choose roll value once per game), Double Move (move two tokens in one turn), Shield Token (protect one token for 3 turns), Teleport (move to any safe square), Stack Breaker (capture one token from a stack), and cooldown enforcement for each.
acceptance_criteria: Given Capture Immunity activated, when an opponent lands on the token this turn, then no capture occurs.
Given Auto-Retrieval activated, when the player has a prisoner, then it is returned without needing to roll 6.
Given Dice Selection activated, when the player selects a value 1–6, then that value is used as the roll.
Given Double Move activated, when a token is moved, then the player may move a second token before turn ends.
Given Shield Token activated on a token, when 3 turns pass, then the shield expires and normal capture rules resume.
Given Teleport activated, when a destination safe square is selected, then the token moves there instantly.
Given Stack Breaker activated on a stack, when applied, then exactly one token is captured and the rest remain.
Given each perk used, when cooldown is active, then the same perk cannot be activated until cooldown expires.

tasks.title: Build in-game perks UI (perk panel, activation, feedback)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want a clear UI for activating perks so that I can use them strategically without confusion.
tasks.description: In-game perk panel showing available perks, cooldown state, and activation flow. Visual feedback on perk use (effect indicator on board). Perk bans in ranked/tournament shown clearly.

states_required.empty: No perks unlocked / perks disabled for match

acceptance_criteria: Given a player's available perks, when the panel is open, then each perk shows name, description, and cooldown state.
Given a perk on cooldown, when displayed, then a cooldown timer shows turns remaining.
Given a perk activated, when confirmed, then a visual indicator on the board communicates the active effect.
Given perks banned in ranked, when the match mode is ranked, then the perk panel is hidden or disabled.

tasks.title: Implement perk progression system (unlock by points, in-game currency)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to earn and unlock perks through play so that progression feels rewarding.
tasks.description: Level-based perk unlock system: accumulate points → unlock perks. Define in-game currency (earned via play, cosmetic-only use). Define daily login rewards. Ensure perks are not pay-to-win.
acceptance_criteria: Given a player reaching a points threshold, when evaluated, then a new perk is unlocked.
Given in-game currency earned via play, when spent, then it can only purchase cosmetics (not gameplay-affecting perks).
Given daily login, when the player logs in, then a daily reward is granted.
Given the unlock system, when reviewed, then no perk provides a competitive advantage that cannot be earned through play.

tasks.title: Implement cosmetics system (themes, token skins, board skins)
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want to customize the look of my game so that it feels personal and expressive.
tasks.description: Cosmetics system supporting: board themes (color schemes, patterns), token skins (shapes, icons), and dice skins. Unlockable via in-game currency or season rewards. Applied per-player (opponent sees your skin).

states_required.success: Shop displayed with equipped items highlighted

acceptance_criteria: Given a player owns a token skin, when equipped, then their tokens appear with the selected skin on the board.
Given a board theme, when applied, then the board renders with the new color scheme.
Given a cosmetic available for purchase, when the player has enough currency, then it can be unlocked.
Given a locked cosmetic, when previewed, then it shows how it looks with an unlock prompt.

tasks.title: Implement achievement system with badges
tasks.user_story: As a player, I want achievements so that milestones feel rewarding and I have long-term goals.
tasks.description: Define achievement list (e.g., first capture, first win, 100 captures, win with all 4 tokens safe, win in team mode). Track progress. Unlock badge on completion. Notify player.
acceptance_criteria: Given a defined achievement, when its condition is met, then it is unlocked and the player is notified.
Given an in-progress achievement, when partially met, then progress percentage is tracked.
Given a newly unlocked achievement, when in-game, then a non-intrusive toast notification appears.
Given the achievements screen, when viewed, then locked and unlocked achievements are displayed.

tasks.title: Implement analytics and telemetry (funnels, gameplay balance, privacy controls)
tasks.user_story: As a developer, I want analytics so that I can understand how players use the game and balance it over time.
tasks.description: Track key funnels (tutorial completion, match start, match completion, churn). Track gameplay balance metrics (win rate by perk, capture rates, average match length). Privacy-aware: no PII, configurable retention, consent banner.
acceptance_criteria: Given a player completing the tutorial, when tracked, then a tutorial_completed event is logged.
Given a match completing, when tracked, then match_completed event includes duration, player count, and ruleset.
Given perk usage, when tracked, then perk_activated events include perk type and match outcome.
Given GDPR/privacy requirements, when the app loads, then a consent banner is shown before analytics begin.
